page: UpCloud PREMIUM-16xCPU-64GB is a general-purpose virtual server hosted on the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor with x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided via a bundled 300 GB local SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks place this server in the average tier for most general compute and memory tasks. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests yielded scores of 1838.78 and 44425.64, respectively. Memory bandwidth tests showed a read-only small block cached speed of 2685.2 GB/sec, while uncached large block read and write speeds reached 147.28 GB/sec and 62.48 GB/sec. However, the server demonstrates top-tier performance in single-threaded gzip compression at 39.49 GB/sec, and strong performance in bzip3 decompression, reaching 173.78 GB/sec in multi-threaded tests.

For specialized workloads, the AMD EPYC processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512, AVX_VNNI, and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. In database and web serving benchmarks, the instance handled 25.98 million Redis SET operations per second and achieved a static web serving throughput of 21.01 GB/sec. LLM inference benchmarks indicate average performance for prompt processing, though text generation speeds on smaller models are relatively low. This resource profile is appropriate for multi-threaded data compression, general-purpose web serving, and moderate database operations.
description: UpCloud PREMIUM-16xCPU-64GB is a general-purpose virtual server utilizing the KVM hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 16 shared vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 9575F processor running at 2.0 GHz, 64.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 300 GB local SSD. Benchmark results show average performance across general compute and memory tasks, with a PassMark CPU score of 35388.02 and a database operations score of 12422.48. Notably, the server excels in data compression, achieving top-tier single-threaded gzip compression speeds of 39.49 GB/sec and strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression at 173.78 GB/sec. It also supports advanced vector and cryptographic instructions like AVX-512 and AES. This configuration is appropriate for general-purpose web serving, multi-threaded data processing, and moderate database workloads.
og_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-16xCPU-64GB is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 300 GB local SSD storage, delivering strong data compression and balanced general-purpose performance.
meta_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-16xCPU-64GB is a KVM virtual server with 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 300 GB SSD storage for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 virtual server featuring sixteen AMD EPYC cores, local SSD storage, and strong data compression capabilities.
bullet_points: 16 AMD EPYC 9575F vCPUs and 64 GB of system memory
Bundled 300 GB local SSD storage and one public IPv4 address
Top-tier single-threaded gzip compression performance at 39.49 GB/sec
Supports AVX-512, AVX_VNNI, and AES cryptographic instructions
Appropriate for general-purpose web serving and moderate database operations
